userStories.title: Refactor schema with metadata columns and primary keys
userStories.description: Rewrite ingest.py to create tables with the 3 metadata columns (_row_hash TEXT, _snapshot_date TEXT, _removed_date TEXT) and proper indexes. Parse TSV headers, identify primary key columns per table (JCAT for most, Launch_Tag+Piece for launchlog). Create tables with all original columns + metadata. Do NOT insert data yet — just the schema creation and TSV parsing logic. Also create a TABLE_KEYS config dict mapping table names to their key column(s).
acceptanceCriteria: Tables created with all original GCAT columns plus _row_hash, _snapshot_date, _removed_date
Indexes on key columns and _snapshot_date per table
TABLE_KEYS dict correctly maps each table to its primary key column(s)
TSV parsing handles comment lines (#), whitespace stripping, and header extraction
Running ingest.py creates empty tables with correct schema

userStories.title: Implement append-only hash-dedup insert logic
userStories.description: Add the core ingest logic: for each row, compute SHA-256 hash of all original fields joined by tab. Look up the latest hash for that key in the DB. If no existing row or hash differs, INSERT with _snapshot_date=today and _row_hash=hash. If hash matches, skip. Use batch operations for performance (load all existing hashes into a dict first, then batch INSERT new/changed rows in a single transaction). Print summary: new, updated, unchanged counts per table.
acceptanceCriteria: SHA-256 computed from all original fields joined by tab separator
Existing hashes loaded in bulk (SELECT key, _row_hash WHERE _snapshot_date = max) into dict
New/changed rows batch-inserted in single transaction
Summary printed: X new, Y updated, Z unchanged per table
Re-running same data twice produces 0 new + 0 updated on second run
Running ingest.py successfully loads all 8 tables with correct row counts

userStories.title: Implement removal detection
userStories.description: After inserting new/changed rows, detect keys that were present in the DB (latest snapshot, _removed_date IS NULL) but are missing from today's source data. For these, UPDATE their latest row to set _removed_date = today's date. Print count of removed records per table.
acceptanceCriteria: Keys present in DB but missing from source get _removed_date set to today
Only the latest version of removed keys is updated (not all historical versions)
Removal count printed per table
A key that reappears in future data gets a new INSERT (not un-removed)

userStories.title: Create convenience views and update query.py
userStories.description: Create SQL views for each table: {table}_latest (current state, excluding removed) and {table}_at(date) pattern documented. Update query.py to list available tables and views on startup in interactive mode. Also add a --tables flag to list all tables with row counts.
acceptanceCriteria: Views created: active_latest, satcat_latest, etc. for all 8 tables
Views correctly show only the latest non-removed version of each record
query.py --tables shows all tables and views with row counts
Interactive mode shows available tables/views on startup

userStories.title: Update README and add sync.sh
userStories.description: Update README.md with the new architecture, example queries (latest state, point-in-time, daily changes, history of an object, growth over time). Create sync.sh that pulls the archive repo and runs ingest. Make it executable.
acceptanceCriteria: README documents append-only hash-dedup approach
README has example queries for all query patterns from PLAN.md
sync.sh pulls archive and runs ingest.py
sync.sh is executable
